sections.subtitle: Recent Form Analysis
sections.text: Real Oviedo has shown mixed form with 2 wins and 3 losses in their last 5 matches, scoring late in games. Levante UD has struggled with only 1 win, 2 draws, and 2 losses in their last 5, but they scored heavily in one match (4-0 vs Girona). Both teams have defensive vulnerabilities, with Oviedo conceding in 4 straight games and Levante in 5 of 7.

sections.subtitle: Head-to-Head Record
sections.text: In the last 2 head-to-head matches, Real Oviedo has a slight edge with 1 win and 1 draw. Matches between these teams tend to be low-scoring, with under 2.5 goals in 6 of their last 8 encounters.

sections.subtitle: Season Statistics
sections.text: Oviedo struggles offensively (4 goals, 19th in league) and defensively (12 conceded, 17th), while Levante has scored more (11 goals) but also conceded heavily (14 goals). Both teams average low corners (around 2.7 per game for Oviedo), and their matches often see moderate card counts.

sections.subtitle: Key Factors
sections.text: Oviedo’s home advantage at Carlos Tartiere and Levante’s inconsistent away form could play a role. Levante’s attacking potential, driven by in-form player Etta Eyong (3 goals recently), contrasts with Oviedo’s defensive frailties. However, head-to-head trends suggest a tight, low-scoring game.

halftimeSections.subtitle: Half-time overview
halftimeSections.text: At the break, Levante UD holds a narrow lead over Real Oviedo with the score at 0-1. The solitary goal came from Carlos Álvarez in the 30th minute, capitalizing on a well-structured attack. Levante UD has shown more clinical finishing in the first half, while Real Oviedo has struggled to convert their possession into meaningful chances. The game remains tightly contested with both teams showing intent, but Levante UD's efficiency in front of goal has made the difference so far.

halftimeSections.subtitle: Attack momentum analysis
halftimeSections.text: Real Oviedo started the match with strong attacking intent, dominating the early phases with consistent pressure in the opposition half. Their momentum was evident in several spells of sustained attack during the opening minutes. However, as the half progressed, Levante UD gradually shifted the balance, particularly in the middle and latter stages, with notable attacking spells that led to their goal. Towards the end of the half, Levante UD maintained a more consistent threat, while Real Oviedo's attacking momentum waned slightly, though they did have brief moments of resurgence.

halftimeSections.subtitle: Team performance breakdown
halftimeSections.text: Real Oviedo showed a decent share of possession and managed several entries into the final third, but their inability to register shots on target is a major concern. Their defensive structure has held up reasonably well, with players like Eric Bailly and Lucas Ahijado contributing significantly at the back. On the other hand, Levante UD has been more effective with their opportunities, managing multiple shots on target and penetrating the penalty area more frequently. Their slightly higher possession and aerial dominance have helped them maintain control for key periods.

halftimeSections.subtitle: Key players at half-time
halftimeSections.text: For Levante UD, Carlos Álvarez has been the standout performer, not only scoring the crucial goal but also posing a constant threat in attack. Manuel Sánchez has also been pivotal with his playmaking from the back, providing the assist for the goal. On Real Oviedo's side, Haissem Hassan and Lucas Ahijado have shown promise with their contributions in attack and defense respectively, though they lack the final product so far. Salomón Rondón has been involved but needs to be more decisive in the box.

halftimeSections.subtitle: Second half expectations
halftimeSections.text: Real Oviedo will likely come out with renewed vigor in the second half, pushing for an equalizer with a more aggressive approach in the final third. They need to capitalize on their set-pieces and improve their finishing to turn the tide. Levante UD, on the other hand, will aim to protect their lead while looking for counter-attacking opportunities to extend it. Given their attacking efficiency in the first half, they might exploit any defensive lapses from Oviedo. The match could open up as Oviedo presses, potentially leading to more goal-scoring chances for both sides.
